intel.title: GTC 2026 Catalyst
intel.body: Jensen Huang confirmed Blackwell Ultra shipments accelerating in H1 2026. Hyperscaler capex commitments from AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud all raised guidance.

intel.title: Blackwell Demand Exceeds Supply
intel.body: NVDA backlog for B200 and GB200 systems extends into Q3 2026. Taiwan fab partners confirmed additional capacity allocation.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $175 Avg PT
intel.body: 38 Buy / 4 Hold / 0 Sell. +47.7% upside. No sell ratings. GTC catalyst + Blackwell ramp makes NVDA the highest-conviction AI infrastructure play for Q2 2026.

intel.title: AI Infrastructure Monopoly
intel.body: NVDA holds ~85% GPU market share for AI training workloads. CUDA ecosystem lock-in creates durable switching costs across all major cloud providers.

intel.title: China Export Controls
intel.body: BIS restrictions on H20 exports limit TAM in China. ~10% of revenue at risk. NVDA has partially offset with Hopper-lite SKUs compliant with current rules.

intel.title: Sovereign AI Wave
intel.body: 16 national AI programs globally are ordering NVDA infrastructure. EU, Japan, India, Saudi Arabia all announced GPU clusters in Q1 2026 — a new demand vector not in consensus models.

thesis: NVDA enters the week with GTC 2026 as a confirmed near-term catalyst. Blackwell Ultra shipments accelerating in H1 2026 while hyperscaler capex commitments from all three major clouds have been raised. RSI at 48 with MACD bullishly expanding and price above VWAP creates a clean technical entry ahead of a fundamental tailwind. The sovereign AI demand wave — 16 national programs ordering NVDA infrastructure — is a non-consensus growth vector that extends the demand runway well beyond current consensus models.

updates.note: T1 hit at open Wednesday. GTC announcement confirmed Blackwell Ultra timeline. Price briefly touched $125.80 before pulling back. T2 at $130.60 remains valid for any remaining position. Stop trailed to entry ($118.50) for risk-free hold.
updates.developments: GTC confirmed Blackwell Ultra shipping to hyperscalers in April. Microsoft Azure announced additional $4B NVDA GPU commitment. Short interest declined 8% week-over-week.

intel.title: Apple Intelligence Monetisation
intel.body: Services revenue run-rate $110B. Apple Intelligence features driving upgrade cycle — iPhone 17 pre-orders tracking 18% above iPhone 16.

intel.title: Moderate Buy · $248 PT
intel.body: 24 Buy / 14 Hold / 2 Sell. Modest +4% upside to consensus PT. Analyst sentiment positive but cautious — China iPhone weakness offsetting domestic strength.

intel.title: China Market Weakness
intel.body: iPhone shipments in China declined 12% YoY in Q1 2026 amid Huawei competition. China represents ~17% of total revenue. Ongoing headwind with no clear catalyst for reversal.

intel.title: EU DMA Compliance Costs
intel.body: EU Digital Markets Act mandates require Apple to open App Store to third-party payments. Estimated $4–6B annual revenue impact if fully implemented in 2026.

intel.title: Services Moat
intel.body: Services segment now 24% of revenue with 78% gross margins. 1B+ active devices provide recurring revenue base that is largely macro-insensitive.

intel.title: Gemini Partnership Upside
intel.body: Apple-Google Gemini deal for AI features creates a royalty stream estimated at $18–20B annually by 2027. This is structurally undermodelled by consensus.

thesis: AAPL offered a technically sound setup with MACD bullish and RSI in recovery zone, but the fundamental case was weaker than other picks this week. China iPhone weakness and EU regulatory headwinds created downside risk that materialised — the stop at $231.80 was hit on March 11 when broader tech sold off on tariff concerns.

updates.note: Stopped out at $231.80 on Tuesday March 11 as broader tech sold off on new tariff headlines. Loss contained to −2.8% as planned. The setup did not invalidate fundamentally — watch for re-entry above $235 with VWAP reclaim.
updates.developments: Tariff uncertainty hit consumer electronics names broadly. AAPL-specific news: iPhone 17 pre-order data remains strong per channel checks. Potential re-entry opportunity forming.

intel.title: Llama 4 + AI Ads Monetisation
intel.body: Meta's Llama 4 launch drove 22% engagement increase on Reels. AI-powered ad targeting improvements contributing to CPM expansion — Q1 2026 ad revenue tracking +21% YoY.

intel.title: Reality Labs Breakeven Path
intel.body: Reality Labs losses narrowing to $1.2B in Q4 2025 vs $4.6B in 2024. Orion AR glasses on track for consumer launch H2 2026 — first hardware product with mass market potential.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $800 PT
intel.body: 42 Buy / 6 Hold / 0 Sell. +16.8% upside. Zero sell ratings. META is the most profitable social media company in history with 81.8% gross margin. FCF $52B.

intel.title: 3.3B Daily Active Users
intel.body: Family of Apps DAP hit 3.3B in Q4 2025, up 5% YoY. Threads at 320M MAUs growing 40% QoQ. WhatsApp Business monetisation just beginning.

intel.title: EU Regulatory Pressure
intel.body: EU ordered Meta to pause EU-US data transfers pending new framework. Minor operational headwind. Historical pattern: EU fines ~$1–2B, META absorbs and continues.

intel.title: AI Agent Platform
intel.body: Meta AI assistant at 700M MAUs — #2 globally behind Google. Potential to become the dominant consumer AI interface across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook for 3B+ users.

thesis: META remains a high-quality AI advertising play with the strongest ad targeting moat in social media. The Llama 4 launch is already translating to measurable engagement and CPM expansion, which flows directly to revenue. At 22.4× fwd P/E with 19% revenue growth and 81.8% gross margins, this is arguably the best quality/price combination in the Magnificent 7. The setup entered Monday and is still active — price action has been orderly and the stop has not been threatened.

updates.note: Position is progressing well. META up +2.4% from entry. Price action is constructive — trading above both the daily EMA9 and VWAP. T1 at $716.80 is within reach. No fundamental reason to exit. Maintain stop at $661.50. Consider trailing if T1 is tagged.
updates.developments: Meta announced WhatsApp Business subscription tier at $20/month — new revenue stream with 200M+ Business users. Llama 4 Maverick benchmark scores confirmed leadership position vs GPT-4o.

intel.title: Google Cloud 48% YoY
intel.body: $240B backlog, operating income +154% YoY in Q4 2025. Wiz $32B acquisition closed. BMO raised Q1 2026 Cloud growth estimate to 40%.

intel.title: Apple Gemini Partnership
intel.body: Gemini models to power iPhone AI features. 750M monthly active users. New consumer monetisation channel worth billions annually.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $377 PT · 0 Sells
intel.body: 32 Buy / 6 Hold / 0 Sell (TipRanks). +22% upside. Only Magnificent 7 stock with unanimous buy/hold consensus.

intel.title: Cheapest Mag7 on Fwd P/E
intel.body: 22.9× fwd P/E despite 18% revenue growth, 59.7% gross margin. Waymo autonomous driving optionality not in consensus models.

intel.title: DOJ Antitrust — Manageable
intel.body: DOJ/states appealing Sept 2025 remedy ruling. Adtech case in remedies phase. No criminal probe. Courts already rejected Chrome/Android structural breakup.

intel.title: $140–185B CapEx 2026 Ramp
intel.body: Near-term FCF compression risk. Consensus treats this as growth investment. Cloud backlog of $240B makes the ROI case compelling over a 2–3 year horizon.

thesis: GOOGL tops the technical ranking (13/20) with MACD in bullish expansion, RSI neutral-constructive at 50.6, and intraday confirmation above VWAP. Cloud at 48% YoY and $240B backlog provide structural upside that 22.9× fwd P/E — cheapest in Magnificent 7 — does not reflect. No earnings within 5 days.

intel.title: Agentforce $800M ARR · +169%
intel.body: 29,000 deals closed (+50% QoQ). $72.4B total RPO +14% YoY. Q4 FY26 revenue +12% — fastest growth in 2 years.

intel.title: $50B Buyback · US Army Contract
intel.body: "Because these are low prices" — Benioff. $4.2B returned Q4. Dividend +5.8%. Active US Army enterprise Agentforce deployment.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $268 PT · +38%
intel.body: 28B / 7H / 1S (TipRanks). BTIG $335: market "pricing in CRM's demise" — fundamentally disconnected from execution.

intel.title: $72B RPO Backlog
intel.body: 18+ months contracted revenue. 60%+ Agentforce bookings from existing customers. $16.4B FCF. Deep structural stickiness.

intel.title: SaaS → Usage Transition Risk
intel.body: Seat-based to usage-based Agentforce pricing creates revenue recognition uncertainty. FY27 guide slightly missed consensus.

intel.title: 13× Fwd P/E · 3-Year Low
intel.body: Historical avg ~25×. $72B RPO + 77.7% gross margin + $50B buyback = asymmetric reward/risk at current valuation.

thesis: CRM posts the highest composite conviction score (71). At 13× fwd P/E — a 3-year low — Agentforce ARR +169% and $72B RPO show the thesis is intact. RSI intraday 32.4 signals short-term oversold on top of the bullish daily MACD. $50B buyback signals management conviction at current prices.

intel.title: Azure 39% · $625B Commercial RPO
intel.body: Intelligent Cloud $32.9B (+29%). $625B commercial RPO +110% including $250B OpenAI commitment. Q3 guide 37–38%.

intel.title: $12.7B Buybacks Q2 · FCF $74B
intel.body: +32% YoY capital return. FCF $74B. M365 90M+ subscribers. 230% commercial bookings growth Q2.

intel.title: Strong Buy · $495 PT · +26%
intel.body: 35B / 7H / 0S. Foundry $1M+ customers +80% QoQ. OpenAI's "provider of scale" — deepest AI lock-in.

intel.title: Copilot ARPU Expansion
intel.body: M365 commercial +17% YoY. E5 + Copilot driving ARPU. Dynamics +18% all workloads. Commercial ex-OpenAI RPO +28%.

intel.title: OpenAI Concentration ~45% RPO
intel.body: ~45% of $625B RPO from OpenAI. CFO Hood flagged quarterly volatility from concentration on earnings call.

intel.title: Beta 0.9 — Safest Overnight
intel.body: Lowest risk of the three picks. Platform flywheel: OS + M365 + Azure + Copilot compounding. Wait for VWAP reclaim.

thesis: MSFT offers the highest-quality fundamental backdrop: Azure 39%, $625B RPO, $74B FCF. RSI 41.1 in recovery zone with MACD bullishly expanding. BB pos 0.20 — lower band mean-reversion setup. RSI intraday 26.0 deep oversold — wait for VWAP reclaim before full size. Beta 0.9 makes this the safest overnight hold.
